steps.title: Find the first area
steps.content:
Circle $M$ has radius $3$ millimeters. Use the area formula to find its area:
$$A_M=\pi(3)^2=9\pi$$

steps.title: Add both circle areas
steps.content:
Circle $N$ already has area $169\pi$ square millimeters. Add the two circle areas:
$$9\pi+169\pi=178\pi$$
The combined area is $178\pi$ square millimeters, which is choice D. The answer is D.
